Jerry mad and walks up through the pocket, don't see nothin' at first, but after a few steps back to the pocket, he spots Tom hidin' behind it. Jerry tries to jump into another corner pocket, but Tom aims a cue ball with so much force that it rolls into the pocket, spins back out and rolls Jerry backwards to Tom, who done made a ramp with his cue stick for the mouse to slide up. Jerry stops at the top of the stick and then gets blown down by Tom, who then shoots a stream of balls to flatten the mouse. The whole train rebounds back towards the cat and the balls stack up at Tom’s end of the table. Tom shoots all the balls in succession with his cue, and then tries to shoot Jerry, but the mouse hangs onto the cue tip. The cat, like he sayin' "Have it your way",
text_b: What Tom do when Jerry see him and try to jump? Shoots a cue ball at him

text_a: Zakariya Essabar, a Moroccan citizen, moved to Germany in February 1997 and to Hamburg in 1998, where he studied medical technology. Soon after moving to Hamburg, Essabar met Binalshibh and the others through a Turkish mosque. Essabar turned extremist fairly suddenly, probably in 1999, and reportedly pressured one acquaintance with physical force to become more religious, grow a beard, and compel his wife to convert to Islam. Essabar's parents were said to have made repeated but unsuccessful efforts to sway him from this lifestyle. Shortly before the 9/11 attacks, he would travel to Afghanistan to communicate the date for the attacks to the al Qaeda leadership. Mounir el Motassadeq, another Moroccan, came to Germany in 1993, moving to Hamburg two years later to study electrical engineering at theTechnical University. A witness has recalled Motassadeq saying that he would kill his entire family if his religious beliefs demanded it. One of Motassadeq's roommates recalls him referring to Hitler as a "good man" and organizing film sessions that included speeches by  Bin Laden. Motassadeq would help conceal the Hamburg group's trip to Afghanistan in late 1999. Abdelghani Mz
text_b: Which country did Essabar and Binalshibh link up in? Afghanistan

text_a: When he got back to Nuremberg in 1495, Durer set up his own shop (had to be married to do that). Over the next five years, his art started mixing in Italian styles with the Northern styles he knew. Durer's daddy passed away in 1502, and his mama in 1513. The best stuff he made early on in his shop were his woodcut prints, mostly about religion, but he also did scenes like The Men's Bath House (around 1496). These cuts were bigger and finer than most German woodcuts before, and they were way more complex and balanced in how they were set up. Folks now think Durer probably didn't cut any of the woodblocks himself; a specialist craftsman would have done that. But, his training in Wolgemut's studio, where they made a lot of carved and painted altarpieces and did the design and cutting for woodcuts, really taught him a lot about what you could do with the technique and how to work with the folks who cut the blocks. Durer either drew his design straight on the woodblock, or he stuck a paper drawing on the block. Either way, his drawings got messed up when they cut the block.
text_b: Whether dem designs was drawn straight on da block or was paper drawings glued to da block, what was da end result of da original work? They was stuck.
